rules: Use 3NF (Third Normal Form) to avoid redundant data and update anomalies
Move repeated groups of attributes into separate tables with foreign keys
Ensure each table has a clear primary key and represents a single entity or relationship
Avoid storing calculated values unless they're expensive to compute
Separate frequently accessed columns from rarely accessed ones when appropriate
Use junction tables for many-to-many relationships
whenToDenormalize: When read performance is critical and writes are infrequent
When calculated values are expensive to compute on-the-fly
When denormalization significantly improves query performance
Always document the reason for denormalization
